title: Neznámý titulek
content: Legendary BJJ competitor Caio Terra has recently opened up about some of the lasting injuries that his career has left him with, and the effects they have on him are shocking. It should come as no surprise that he will be dealing with some damage given that he spent decades training hard as an elite combat sports athlete, but the impact it has on him is pretty severe. Most athletes suffer injuries during their careers but Jiu-Jitsu is a whole different beast, as every match involves both parties actively attempting to put each other at risk of injury in order to get the win. Terra revealed that even years after retirement, he still deals with some of this injuries today: “My shoulders are really bad, both of them. And today I can’t do things like brushing my teeth or fixing my hair for long because my shoulders are already hurting. And that’s something that messes with my life.” Terra competed at the very highest level of the sport for the better part of a decade and regularly entered absolutes as one of the lightest in the bracket, so sustaining injuries was always going to happen. According to Terra, he thinks at least some of the damage he’s dealing with might have been preventable: “What I didn’t realize is that maybe as a professional athlete, I should do rehab on my joints because now they’re so weak because I never did it. I think that I did get better because I was weaker and it forced me to learn Jiu-Jitsu. But at the same time, I think that my body suffers now because of that.” It isn’t just injuries suffered in competition though, many injuries for top competitors are actually suffered or at least exacerbated in the training room instead. Terra hinted at that himself when he reflected on his career: “I wish that I trained a little bit less, maybe 70% of what I trained because I think that would still be a good amount of hours on the mat for me to continue to evolve.” Clips from the interview with Caio Terra where he discusses the injuries he lives with was uploaded to the official Instagram account of Dave Meyer: View this post on Instagram Tags: Caio Terra
